I am glad to share that I have completed the “AI Agents and Agentic AI in Python: Powered by Generative AI” specialization with Vanderbilt University. 

It included 3 courses covering building agents from scratch, frameworks, principles, and architectures for building reliable agents, and prompt engineering, so wonderfully taught by Dr. Jules White .
...
And the result is a new thinking about how I can use Agents and LLMs to make my work more efficient and creative and find new solutions that were not possible before.  :-)
...
All of us are very familiar with LLMs generating and analyzing code, but here are some other approaches that I am using at the beginning of a new data science project
...
·      Use LLM as SME persona to brainstorm which data could be helpful for predicting the target and why, and what are the possible definitions of success for the model implementation
...
·      Create additional data by extracting information from unstructured documents and analyzing images 
·      Search for the research papers surveying different methods of solving a particular modeling problem and model performance benchmarks
...
I am excited to share that I’ve completed the last course from the AI Agents specialization: "Prompt Engineering for ChatGPT"!
...
I have completed the third course from AI Agents specialization: AI Agents and Agentic AI Architecture in Python with Vanderbilt university!
...
This GEN AI Bootcamp session involved building Streamlit chatbots on Snowflake allowing querying both structured and unstructured data using natural language interface. I was especially interested to know LLM translate human language into SQL queries and the part involving Semantic Models was just about that! 

I am sure that for many people like me, who consider SQL their native language, it is fascinating to see how the technology and the world are evolving, so it's important to keep abreast :-)
...
I built my new Coursera course, Generative AI & AI Agent Organizational Strategy for Leaders, because we don't have generations to figure this out. The organizations that learn to capture and amplify their expertise through AI will define the next era.

# Union Pacific Railroad Data Scientist Salary
...
The average Union Pacific Railroad Data Scientist earns an estimated $137,881 annually, which includes an estimated base salary of $116,544 with a $21,337 bonus. Union Pacific Railroad's Data Scientist compensation is $8,376 more than the US average for a Data Scientist. Data Scientist salaries at Union Pacific Railroad can range from $47,500 - $280,000.
...

General Director - Tech and Waybill Solutions (Customer Care & Support) at [Union Pacific Railroad](https://www.linkedin.com/company/unionpacific)
...
As the General Director of Tech and Waybill Solutions at Union Pacific Railroad, I oversee the customer care and support functions for the largest railroad network in North America. With over five years of experience in data analytics and reporting, I deliver innovative and effective solutions to enhance customer satisfaction, operational efficiency, and business performance. I have a Master of Management of Information Systems from the University of Nebraska at Omaha and multiple Tableau certifications. I have expertise in Snowflake, RPA, web development, and various database languages. I am passionate about promoting data literacy and citizen automation across the enterprise, and enabling data-driven decision making and value creation. I am also committed to fostering a culture of collaboration, learning, and excellence among my team and stakeholders.

results.title: Joshua Ford
highlights:
SAP Transformation Leader at [Union Pacific Railroad](https://www.linkedin.com/company/unionpacific)
...
As VP of Product Engineering at PS Technology, I lead talented teams in developing cutting-edge software for the railroad industry. My journey began at Brigham Young University, where I earned a BS in Operations Management and later completed an MBA with a focus on Supply Chain Management. During my academic pursuit, I spent two impactful years in Australia aiding Asian refugees/immigrants, mastering Vietnamese, and delved into business and manufacturing across China, Singapore, Japan, Hong Kong, and Vietnam. My endeavors led to earning a Global Management Certificate. Certified as an APICS Supply Chain Professional (CSCP) and Production & Inventory Management Expert (CPIM), I've contributed to the Midlands Chapter Board of Directors. My career spans food production, semiconductor manufacturing, and a 17+ year tenure at Union Pacific Railroad. At Union Pacific, I spearheaded heavily customized SAP implementation projects in asset management and services procurement. At PS Technology, a subsidiary of Union Pacific, I led the development and delivery of 'Precision Train Builder,' a game-changing rail industry solution for in-train force simulations, enhancing operational efficiency and preventing derailments. Additionally, I guided 'Mobile Tie-Up,' streamlining rail-crew management and leading technical services engagements with shortline railroads. Throughout my journey, I've successfully led teams in application development and support across Engineering, Supply Chain, Finance, and Human Resources, driving impactful transformations in these domains.
...

...
#### Senior Systems Engineer (Data Platforms Team) (Current)
...
The prior team lead left; I was fortune enough to be chosen to backfill the position. Essentially I will be doing what I have done the past year with more responsibilities to lead and own the system(s). "As a lead in the SHIELD/NoSQL area you will play a crucial role in designing and implementing scalable and efficient data platforms that meet our organization's evolving needs. The successful candidate must be a highly motivated and creative individual with strong technical skills, who is able to lead a matrixed team to deliver complex solutions based on Union Pacific’s business needs"
...
#### System Engineer (Data Platforms Team) (Current)
...
Accepted a position within the Data Platforms team within the Technology department. Specifically supporting the NoSQL infrastructure as a Data Platform Engineer that has responsibility to host critical data support and availability for the entire company. This is a shift from my existing app development expertise and a huge chance to grow my skillset/ expertise in a new challenging way. The Data Platforms team is responsible for all mission critical transactional data systems at Union Pacific including RDBMS’s and various NoSQL data platforms as well as matrixed responsibility for Cloud data architecture, platforms, and governance that support our Cloud Data Strategy. The position will be responsible for products within the NoSQL and SHIELD product lines which could include Coherence, Solr, OpenSearch, Neo4j, App Management Orchestration, Zookeeper, and others. As a systems engineer in the NoSQL area, I will play a crucial role in designing and implementing scalable and efficient data platforms that meet our organization's evolving needs.

The goal was to take a picture of a railroad equipment and identify the equipment for input into application fields when filling out data entry. results.title: Venkat Nikhil K
highlights:
Gen AI Engineer
...
Senior Gen AI Engineer and Lead engineer with 10+ years of experience designing, building, and optimizing LLM-enabled data platforms across AWS, GCP, Azure, and Snowflake environments. Strong expertise in Big Data ecosystems including Hadoop, Spark, Hive, Kafka, Airflow, and dbt, with a focus on building AI/LLM-ready data architectures. Proven experience developing end-to-end ELT/ETL pipelines for structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data, enabling Generative AI, RAG-based analytics, and intelligent data applications at enterprise scale. Hands-on experience with Snowflake data warehousing, including Snowpipe, Streams, Tasks, performance tuning, cost optimization, data governance, and secure data access for AI workloads. Advanced proficiency in Python, PySpark, SQL, and Unix scripting, with experience building LLM-integrated pipelines, AI-driven validation frameworks, and intelligent automation workflows. Extensive experience with workflow orchestration using Apache Airflow, Cloud Composer, and dbt Cloud, supporting AI-driven data processing and real-time analytics. Collaborated with data scientists to productionize AI/ML models and enable LLM-powered applications, supporting both real-time and batch inference pipelines. Expertise in data ingestion from RDBMS, APIs, and streaming platforms using Kafka, Sqoop, Flume, and Pub/Sub, building event-driven architectures for AI and LLM use cases. Experience building real-time and batch processing pipelines using Spark, Dataflow, and Kafka Streams, enabling low-latency data delivery for AI/LLM systems. Strong understanding of data quality, governance, and AI-ready validation frameworks, ensuring reliable and audit-compliant datasets. Built and optimized data pipelines to support AI/ML and LLM workloads, ensuring high-quality, scalable, and low-latency feature delivery. Proven ability to collaborate with business stakeholders, offshore teams, and cross-functional engineering teams to deliver AI-enabled and Agentic AI-driven data solutions, leveraging Python, AWS Bedrock, LangChain, LangGraph, and prompt engineering. Experience across healthcare, finance, insurance, life sciences, and ad-tech domains, delivering LLM-integrated data platforms and Agentic AI workflows supporting advanced analytics and intelligent data applications.

highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
...
# Data Scientist
...
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
...
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
...
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
...
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
...
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics, machine learning, simulation, and mathematical modelling
...
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
...
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
...
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and leadership to support data driven decision making
...
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
...
### Required Qualifications:
...
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
...
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
...
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
...
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence
...
Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.

results.title: Lead AI Engineer, Biomedical & Vigilance Innovation - Xtalks
highlights:
## Lead AI Engineer, Biomedical & Vigilance Innovation
...
California, US residents click here.The job details are as follows:Who We AreWe are the first publicly-traded biotech or pharmaceutical company to take the form of a public benefit corporation. Our public benefit purpose is to provide a brighter future for patients through the development of novel pharmaceutical therapies; and technologies that expand the availability of transplantable organs.United Therapeutics (Nasdaq: UTHR) seeks to travel down the corridors of indifference to develop treatments for rare, deadly diseases. We were founded in 1996 by a family seeking a cure for their daughter's pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH). Today, we have six FDA-approved therapies that treat PAH, pulmonary hypertension associated with interstitial lung disease (PH-ILD) and neuroblastoma, a rare pediatric cancer. Our near-term pipeline seeks to develop additional therapies for PAH and pulmonary fibrosis (PF).The cure for end-stage life-threatening diseases like PAH, PH-ILD, PF, and many others is an organ transplant, but only a small percentage of donated organs are available to address the vast need. For this reason, we are working to create manufactured organs to address the shortage of kidneys, hearts, lungs, and livers available for transplant. We believe an unlimited supply of tolerable, transplantable organs will eliminate the transplant waiting list and cure end-stage organ diseases for which transplant is not currently an option.Who You AreWe are seeking a Lead AI Engineer, Biomedical & Vigilance Innovation with a start-up mindset to drive the design, development, and deployment of advanced AI solutions that transform how biomedical insights are generated and how safety signals are detected, assessed, and acted upon, as well as deploying artificial intelligence solutions that strengthen biomedical research, patient safety, and next-generation vigilance capabilities.This role operates at the intersection of data science, software engineering, and medical safety, applying advanced analytics, machine learning, and automation signal detection, case intelligence, business efficiency, and evidence generation from various sources of scientific and RWD. The position will partner closely with safety physicians, clinical scientists, epidemiologists, regulatory leaders, and technology teams to create scalable platforms that support proactive risk management and accelerate informed decision-making across the product lifecycle. This role is instrumental in shaping the future state of digital vigilance and precision medicine.
...
- Design, build, validate, and maintain machine learning, natural language processing, and generative AI solutions for biomedical and pharmacovigilance use cases

Sr Data Scientist /Machine Learning Engineer | Careerport

...
# Sr Data Scientist /Machine Learning Engineer

Remote, USA

Posted 2026-06-16

Full-time

 Overview UP.Labs is a dynamic venture studio dedicated to building innovative startup companies from the ground up. Our team thrives on solving complex problems, driving technological advancements, and creating impactful digital products. We're seeking a highly skilled professional to join our growing team and contribute to our mission of launching the next wave of successful startups. Technical Challenge As a Data Scientist, you will leverage advanced analytics and machine learning techniques to extract actionable insights from complex datasets and build predictive models that drive strategic decision-making. You will work with cutting-edge technologies to develop data-driven solutions that power our portfolio of mobility-focused ventures. Responsibilities Design, develop, and deploy machine learning models and data science solutions to solve complex business problems across our venture portfolio Build and maintain scalable data pipelines and analytics infrastructure using Python and Azure cloud services Implement generative AI solutions and integrate large language models into products to enhance user experiences and automate workflows Collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into technical data science solutions Conduct exploratory data analysis, feature engineering, and model evaluation to ensure high-quality, production-ready solutions Required Skills Strong expertise in data science and machine learning methodologies, with demonstrated ability to build and deploy models in production environments Advanced proficiency in Python programming for data analysis, machine learning, and automation Solid experience with generative AI technologies and frameworks, including prompt engineering and LLM integration Working knowledge of Microsoft Azure cloud platform for deploying and scaling data science solutions Experience with modern data science libraries and frameworks such as scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, or similar tools Strong understanding of statistical analysis, time series analysis, experimental design, and data visualization techniques Preferred Skills Experience with geospatial analysis using tools such as ESRI ArcGIS products or geopandas UP.Labs Summary We build high-growth technology startups that enable faster, cleaner, and safer movement of people and goods. Our vision is to transform the moving world by pairing leading corporations and entrepreneurs with a proven methodology for launching and scaling software and hardware companies. We work with corporate investors over a multi-year period to launch a portfolio of mobility-focused ventures. Our team is dedicated to the first year of a new venture's life cycle, from ideation to minimum viable product build (and beyond) to recruiting and hiring the full-time team who will scale the business. Location: Remote

We are looking for an Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning, Vice President with a robust background in Generative AI, Machine Learning, enterprise architecture, cloud platforms, and scalable solution design. You will be responsible for designing and implementing AI-enabled solutions that are secure, resilient, compliant, and aligned with enterprise architecture standards. This role requires strong experience in translating business requirements into end-to-end AI solution architectures, including platform integration, governance, observability, and production readiness. The ideal candidate will work across business, engineering, security, data, and risk teams to deliver scalable and responsible AI solutions for enterprise use cases.

This role is important because it helps ensure AI solutions are designed for production readiness, compliance, interoperability, and long-term business value across the organization.
...

...
• Design end-to-end AI/ML and Generative AI solution architectures for enterprise use cases, including model integration, orchestration, APIs, data flows, and downstream system interactions.

...

...

...

...

...
• Partner with platform, cloud, security, and DevSecOps teams to ensure production readiness, observability, access control, and operational excellence.
...
